_comment: Creator OS connector registry + feature-flag contract. Each connector declares the normalized EVIDENCE it can provide so an atom can use whatever is available and DEGRADE via alternates when one is off. Offline contract only — we build NO provider auth/API clients here. Live retrieval is realized by the host AI's native integration (Claude / OpenAI / Gemini) when the deployment has connected it, or via manual_paste / uploaded_file. A deployment may RESTRICT specific evidence even when a connector is active (see per-deployment 'restricted_evidence' + reason); atoms then take what they can get and look elsewhere. Prose: connectors.md. Per-deployment flags: feature-flags.example.json.

evidence_modes.use_when: a platform API or native integration is available and fresh data is needed
no live connector but the creator has exported files
inputs: uploaded_file: csv/json exports, media, zip bundles
evidence_modes.primary_weakness: loses platform-level metadata only the API returns

evidence_modes.use_when: only pasted text or a partial excerpt exists
evidence_modes.primary_weakness: no validation against the original artifact; weaker timing and identity confidence

evidence_modes.use_when: no new external evidence; only existing records
required_transforms: never imply retrieval or platform certainty
evidence_modes.primary_weakness: cannot support claims needing fresh external data

evidence_modes.use_when: several partial sources exist and gaps must be filled across them
inputs: any mix of the other modes
evidence_modes.primary_weakness: requires explicit conflict handling and freshness checks

evidence_mode_rules.silent_downgrade_forbidden: every step down the ladder records the confidence lost in the execution trace
evidence_mode_rules.hybrid_conflict_policy: conflicting sources produce a minority-report conflict entry, never an average

connectors.label: Manual paste
connectors.auth_note: none — creator pastes data directly into the conversation

connectors.label: Uploaded file (read via shared/docintel/)
connectors.auth_note: none — creator uploads .pdf/.docx/.csv/.xlsx; read via docintel-engine and parse_text.py

connectors.label: Local .local.json data files (voice-profile, channel-context, content-calendar)
connectors.auth_note: none — reads pipeline/user-context/*.local.json; gitignored, never committed. Created by tools/setup.py.

connectors.label: YouTube Data API v3
connectors.auth_note: OAuth 2.0 or API key via Google Cloud Console. Quota: 10,000 units/day default. Realized by host AI's native Google integration or via integrations-engine.md credential pattern. Enable by setting youtube_api: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Instagram Graph API (Meta) v25.0
connectors.auth_note: Instagram Business or Creator account required. OAuth 2.0 via Meta app. Scopes: instagram_business_basic, instagram_business_content_publish, instagram_business_manage_insights. Enable by setting instagram_api: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: TikTok APIs (Content Posting, Research, Display)
connectors.auth_note: TikTok developer account required. Login Kit for OAuth 2.0. Content Posting API: video.publish or video.upload scope. Research API: non-profit/academic only. Enable by setting tiktok_api: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Pinterest API v5
connectors.auth_note: Pinterest Developer account required (read side). OAuth 2.0. Scopes: pins:read, boards:read. Rate limits: reads 1,000/min. Enable by setting pinterest_api: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: YouTube Publishing (Data API v3 upload)
connectors.auth_note: Write side. OAuth 2.0 with the youtube.upload scope and a user refresh token (obtained via the wizard's YouTube OAuth flow). Separate from the read-only youtube_api flag. Enable by setting youtube_publishing: true in creator-os-config.local.json and adding credentials to pipeline/user-context/api-credentials.local.json. Live network calls are gated behind live_publishing_enabled (tools/publishing/).

connectors.label: Instagram Publishing (Graph API v25.0 Reels)
connectors.auth_note: Write side. Meta app with the instagram_business_content_publish scope + a long-lived access token and the IG Business account id. Separate from the read-only instagram_api flag. Enable by setting instagram_publishing: true in creator-os-config.local.json. Live network calls are gated behind live_publishing_enabled (tools/publishing/).

connectors.label: TikTok Publishing (Content Posting API)
connectors.auth_note: Write side. Login Kit OAuth with the video.publish scope; supports the AIGC disclosure flag. No native scheduled_at (the dashboard's background scheduler dispatches). Separate from the read-only tiktok_api flag. Enable by setting tiktok_publishing: true in creator-os-config.local.json. Live network calls are gated behind live_publishing_enabled (tools/publishing/).

connectors.label: Pinterest Publishing (API v5 create-pin)
connectors.auth_note: Write side. OAuth 2.0 with the pins:write and boards:write scopes; write rate limit 100/min. Separate from the read-only pinterest_api flag. Enable by setting pinterest_publishing: true in creator-os-config.local.json. Live network calls are gated behind live_publishing_enabled (tools/publishing/).

connectors.label: FCPXML / OTIO file interchange
connectors.auth_note: No account. The universal file bridge: generate/parse FCPXML (.fcpxmld) and OpenTimelineIO. Works on any OS since it is just a file; Final Cut Pro and DaVinci Resolve both read FCPXML. Realized by tools/videoedit/fcpxml.py + otio_core.py. Validation via xmllint against the DTD in the installed editor. Enable by setting video_editing_enabled: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: DaVinci Resolve Scripting API (Studio)
connectors.auth_note: Live NLE control on macOS/Windows/Linux via the Resolve Python API. REQUIRES DaVinci Resolve Studio (paid); the free version cannot be scripted externally. Env vars RESOLVE_SCRIPT_API / RESOLVE_SCRIPT_LIB set from the install; pin Python 3.10 to 3.12. Realized by tools/videoedit/resolve.py; degrades to fcpxml_interchange when Studio/Resolve is absent. Enable by setting resolve_scripting: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Apple Compressor (command line)
connectors.auth_note: macOS batch encoder. Runs platform export presets (.compressorsetting) via /Applications/Compressor.app/Contents/MacOS/Compressor. Presets keyed to shared/platform-engine.md. Cross-OS alternative is the Resolve render queue. Enable by setting compressor_presets: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: CommandPost (FCP UI automation)
connectors.auth_note: macOS Lua automation layer for Final Cut Pro finishing chores FCPXML cannot reach (apply LUT, drop intro, run export preset). Realized by tools/videoedit/commandpost.py (emits/triggers macros). Enable by setting commandpost_macros: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Google Drive / Google Workspace
connectors.auth_note: Realized by host AI's native Google Drive integration when the deployment has connected it. Read AND write (Drive can save files back), so it hosts the P35 task store: the canonical task-register JSON plus a Sheets mirror live in Drive and claude.ai web/mobile and Claude Desktop share one store (event-log merge keeps concurrent surfaces consistent). See shared/tasks-engine.md and shared/integrations-engine.md. No client built here; the host connector performs the I/O.

connectors.label: Offline SQLite caches (keyword FTS5 + competitor snapshots)
connectors.auth_note: none — reads shared/cache/index.local.db (FTS5 keyword cache) and pipeline/competitor-snapshots/index.local.db (competitor metadata). Both gitignored. Built by tools/setup.py and tools/competitor_snapshot.py.

connectors.label: Creator OS MCP server (stdio, Claude Desktop)
connectors.auth_note: none — stdio transport. Requires pip install mcp + claude_desktop_config.json entry. Exposes 8 tools: cache_query, competitor_scan, source_staleness, drift_check, quality_score, add_competitor, get_capabilities, get_connectors. Enable by setting mcp_server: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Playwright Chromium (JS rendering for competitor snapshots)
connectors.auth_note: pip install -r requirements-render.txt + playwright install chromium. On macOS M2: auto-downloads arm64 binary. Enables ytInitialData extraction from YouTube pages that require JavaScript. Enable by setting playwright: true in creator-os-config.local.json.

connectors.label: Web crawl (acquire.py + fetch_resilient.py, polite rate-governed)
connectors.auth_note: none — pip install -r requirements-crawl.txt + requirements-scraper.txt. Rate governor respects robots.txt and Crawl-delay. No API key required for basic crawl. Always available if requirements-crawl.txt is installed.

connectors.label: Gmail (Google Workspace)
connectors.auth_note: Enabled via the wizard (tools/wizard.py) or claude.ai Settings > Integrations > Google Workspace. Claude Desktop: workspace-mcp via uvx. claude.ai Projects: native Google Workspace connector (zero setup). Set google_workspace: true in creator-os-config.local.json after connecting.

connectors.label: Google Calendar (Google Workspace)
connectors.auth_note: Same auth as gmail — both are enabled together by the google_workspace capability flag. The workspace-mcp server exposes Calendar read/write alongside Gmail and Drive.

connectors.label: Google Drive / Docs / Sheets (Google Workspace)
connectors.auth_note: Same auth as gmail — Drive covers Docs and Sheets. Analytics tracking sheets and brand brief documents flow in as document_source and spreadsheet_data. Enabled via google_workspace capability flag.

connectors.label: Outlook Email (Microsoft 365)
connectors.auth_note: Enabled via the wizard (tools/wizard.py) for Claude Desktop. Uses Softeria ms-365-mcp-server (npx -y @softeria/ms-365-mcp-server). Device Code Flow: user visits microsoft.com/devicelogin and enters a short code — no redirect server or credentials to manage. Requires Node.js 20+. Set microsoft_365: true in creator-os-config.local.json after connecting.

connectors.label: Outlook Calendar (Microsoft 365)
connectors.auth_note: Same auth as microsoft_outlook_email — all Microsoft 365 services are enabled together by the microsoft_365 capability flag via the Softeria MCP server.

connectors.label: Excel / OneDrive (Microsoft 365)
connectors.auth_note: Same auth as microsoft_outlook_email — the Softeria ms-365-mcp-server covers Excel, OneDrive, Outlook, Calendar, and 200+ Microsoft 365 tools from a single connection.

connectors.label: Wolfram Alpha MCP (exact symbolic computation)
connectors.auth_note: WOLFRAM_APP_ID env var required. Free tier: 2,000 calls/month. Install via uvx mcp-wolfram-alpha.

connectors.label: E2B Code Interpreter (sandboxed Python with scipy/numpy/pandas/matplotlib)
connectors.auth_note: E2B_API_KEY env var required. Cloud-sandboxed execution. No local dependencies beyond the MCP client.

connectors.label: Stats Compass MCP (50+ pandas-based statistical tools)
connectors.auth_note: No API key required. pip install stats-compass-mcp. Local execution.

connectors.label: DuckDB MCP (analytical SQL on CSV/Parquet/JSON)
connectors.auth_note: No API key for local mode. motherduckdb/mcp-server-motherduck. Optional MotherDuck token for cloud mode.

connectors.label: Jupyter Notebook MCP (persistent notebook sessions)
connectors.auth_note: No API key required. pip install jupyter-mcp-server. Runs local Jupyter kernel.

connectors.label: R Statistics MCP (rmcp, 52 R statistical tools)
connectors.auth_note: R must be installed on the system. pip install rmcp.

connectors.label: Monte Carlo Simulation MCP (probabilistic modeling)
connectors.auth_note: No API key required. MCS-MCP MCP server.

connectors.label: scikit-learn MCP (ML classification, regression, clustering)
connectors.auth_note: No API key required. mcp-server-scikit-learn.

connectors.label: EasyPost shipment tracking
connectors.auth_note: API key via HTTP Basic; read from the EASYPOST_API_KEY environment variable only, never persisted. tools/shipments.py polls over stdlib urllib honoring the env proxy + CA bundle (no webhook). Enable by setting shipment_tracking: true in creator-os-config.local.json and providing the key in the environment.

connectors.label: Ship24 shipment tracking
connectors.auth_note: Bearer API key from the SHIP24_API_KEY environment variable only. Free tier ~10 shipments/month. Alternative to EasyPost; same normalized schema in tools/shipments.py. Enable via shipment_tracking: true and the env key.
